title: What an American thinks about Albania

transcript.text: What an american thinks about albania um I was in albania for like a week
Back in the summer of 2010 um I really liked albania albania was a town that
Um was a country that impressed me a lot they were really really really outgoing like insanely
Outgoing it's kind of like morocco in that way only I didn't feel a lot threatened like I did
In morocco like I'd be riding my bike down the road across the country and be people be like
Like I was a celebrity maybe they just never see tourists there tourists avoid albania and it's
Really pretty I got stamped in from the macedonian side I rode my bike from um scotche macedonia to
Tyrana albania over this um you know over this mountains and then we got stamped into albania
And there were it was kind of sparsely populated little little little village here little village
There and then I went over and down some beautiful um road it was kind of like really like red rock
It was like the american american southwest like um like new mexico or something and uh
Yeah went down a really big downhill descent into some town but like I fucked up the signs and I went
The wrong way and um but I rode down and it was pretty bad roads but it was really basic like there
Were people um uh just tilling the soil like it was hundreds of years ago like farms everywhere and
The person lives in their farm and they're working their farm with their simple hand tools it was like
A bunch of hippies living the commune lifestyle yeah um and uh but they were really like waving to me and
I I got lost I was at one town I wanted to know which way it was to get out of the town in a certain

Just standing around it was like the ghetto of a poor country you know like the poor people in the

That's all these people just standing around hanging out um and um yeah but um yeah so then the next day I
Rode in in that morning and it was really bad roads really bumpy but uh tirana is a uh they're

Together in my rack and the guy didn't even want to charge me I was like how much is it oh don't
Worry about it I went into the um the uh internet place internet cafe with all the computers and I was
There like 15 minutes and I was the only guy who had been there the whole time and I was like how
Much is it they're like oh you're only here 15 minutes don't worry about it and then I was at
The hostel hostel was a cool awesome place it was one of the coolest what is it called tirana hostel
Um hangout place you know an outdoor area a nice warm area hanging out you can drink out there and
Party and it was awesome man um yeah the girl who worked there was really cool oh what's her name
Oh my god I'm sorry I should remember your name I'm drunk my brain's not working malvina
Malvina um and uh yeah the other dude working there he wanted to my rack broke and he just helped me fix

As an american um

For some of those other countries in those areas like especially bosnia and serbia
They picked their trash up except for in the city I saw one river and there was trash
Plastic trash bags and stuff all over the place but the rest of it it was really well picked up
I went and asked some got some I asked some dude where I could buy some soap he was working a kiosk
And he led me to some place where I could buy soap and he goes what are you doing in albania and I was

But um but yeah I couldn't believe how outgoing albanians were
They didn't really know that good of english and they have really weird mannerisms kind of like the greeks

I mean I went through some towns and I'd look at the roads over there and it looked like

But uh but they were really friendly too and some of them could speak really good english so
Yeah but I liked elbania I thought it was cool they have some weird stuff going on though like
